- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) on a cron schedule or once at a specific time. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring or one-time remote agent ("run this every Monday", "open a cleanup PR for X in 2 weeks"), or to manage existing routines. ALSO OFFER PROACTIVELY: after you finish work that has a natural future follow-up, end your reply with a one-line offer to schedule a background agent to do it. Strong signals: a feature flag / gate / experiment / staged rollout was just shipped (offer a one-time agent in ~2 weeks to open a cleanup PR or evaluate results), a new alert/monitor was created (offer a recurring agent to triage it), a TODO/migration with a "remove once X" condition was left behind (offer a one-time agent to do the removal). Skip the offer for refactors, bug fixes, and anything that is done once it ships. Name a concrete action and cadence ("in 2 weeks", "every Monday") and only offer when the run just succeeded — do not pitch a schedule for something that has not happened yet.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

from odoo import models, fields, api
from .graph_api import GraphAPIClient
import logging
_logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

class MailActivityTag(models.Model):
    """ 
    Simple Categorization:
    Defines tags like 'Urgent', 'IT', or 'Maintenance' so users can 
    quickly filter the ticket list.
    """
    _name = 'mail.activity.tag'
    _description = 'Activity Tag'

    name = fields.Char(string='Tag Name', required=True)
    color = fields.Integer(string='Color Index')

class MailActivityUpdate(models.Model):
    """ 
    Audit Log:
    A permanent record of progress notes. This is where the notes 
    from the wizard end up after the wizard itself is deleted.
    """
    _name = 'mail.activity.update'
    _description = 'Activity Update Log'
    _order = 'date desc'

    activity_id = fields.Many2one(
        'mail.activity',
        string='Activity',
        required=True,
        ondelete='cascade'
    )
    date = fields.Datetime(
        string='Date',
        default=fields.Datetime.now,
        required=True
    )
    user_id = fields.Many2one(
        'res.users',
        string='User',
        default=lambda self: self.env.user,
        required=True
    )
    note = fields.Html(string='Update Note')
    attachment_ids = fields.Many2many(
        'ir.attachment',
        'mail_activity_update_attachment_rel',
        'update_id',
        'attachment_id',

from odoo import _, api, fields, models


class MailActivity(models.Model):
    _inherit = 'mail.activity'

    def action_open_as_wizard(self):
        self.ensure_one()
        return {
            'name': 'Schedule Activity',
            'type': 'ir.actions.act_window',
            'res_model': 'mail.activity',
            'res_id': self.id,
            'view_mode': 'form',
            # This is the standard popup view used by Odoo
            'view_id': self.env.ref('mail.mail_activity_view_form_popup').id,
            'target': 'new', # This forces the popup
        }

<odoo>
    <record id="view_department_form_inherit_custom" model="ir.ui.view">
        <field name="name">hr.department.form.inherit.custom</field>
        <field name="model">hr.department</field>
        <field name="inherit_id" ref="hr.view_department_form"/>
        <field name="arch" type="xml">
            <xpath expr="//sheet" position="inside">
                <notebook closable="0">
                    <page string="Tickets" name="activities_table_tab">
                        <field name="activity_ids" 
                               context="{
                                   'default_res_model': 'hr.department', 
                                   'default_res_model_id': %(hr.model_hr_department)d, 
                                   'default_res_id': id,
                                   'mail_create_nosubscribe': True,
                                   'mail_create_nolog': True,
                               }">
                            <list decoration-muted="custom_state == 'done'" 
                                  decoration-danger="custom_state == 'cancelled'"
                                  default_order="date_deadline desc">
                                <button name="action_open_as_wizard" type="object" icon="fa-external-link" string="Open" title="Open Activity Wizard" class="btn-primary" context="{'form_view_ref': 'mail.mail_activity_view_form_popup', 'target': 'new'}"/>
                                <field name="res_model" column_invisible="True"/>
                                <field name="res_model_id" column_invisible="True"/>
                                <field name="activity_type_id"/>
                                <field name="summary"/>
                                <field name="target_department_id" placeholder="Select Department..." column_invisible="context.get('default_res_model') != 'hr.department'"/>
                                <field name="target_document_item_id" placeholder="Select Document..."/>
                                <field name="custom_state" widget="badge" decoration-success="custom_state == 'done'" decoration-info="custom_state == 'planned'" decoration-danger="custom_state == 'cancelled'"/>
                                <field name="date_deadline"/>
                                <field name="user_id"/>
                            </list>
                        </field>
                    </page>
                </notebook>
            </xpath>
        </field>
    </record>

    <record id="mail_activity_view_form_popup_inherit_dept" model="ir.ui.view">
        <field name="name">mail.activity.view.form.popup.inherit.dept</field>
        <field name="model">mail.activity</field>
        <field name="inherit_id" ref="mail.mail_activity_view_form_popup"/>
        <field name="arch" type="xml">
            
            <xpath expr="//field[@name='summary']" position="after">
                <field name="custom_state" invisible="1"/>
                <group invisible="activity_category != 'meeting'" string="Meeting Schedule">
                    <field name="calendar_start" 
                           required="activity_category == 'meeting' and custom_state == 'planned'"/>
                    <field name="calendar_stop" 
                           required="activity_category == 'meeting' and custom_state == 'planned'"/>
                    <field name="calendar_event_id" string="Linked Event" readonly="1" invisible="not calendar_event_id"/>
                </group>
            </xpath>

            <xpath expr="//field[@name='summary']" position="attributes">
                <attribute name="required">activity_category == 'meeting'</attribute>
            </xpath>

            <!-- Create Meeting / Reset to Planned are added by activity_modification -->

            <xpath expr="//button[@name='action_done']" position="attributes">
                <attribute name="invisible">custom_state != 'planned'</attribute>
            </xpath>
            <xpath expr="//button[@name='action_done_schedule_next']" position="attributes">
                <attribute name="invisible">custom_state != 'planned'</attribute>
            </xpath>

        </field>
    </record>
</odoo>

Now I understand the full circular dependency:

```
ticket_system → hr_employee_growth → employees_department_custom_fields → ticket_system
ticket_system → hr_employee_certifications → employees_department_custom_fields → ticket_system
```

The cause: `employees_department_custom_fields` was given `ticket_system` as a dep (on this branch) because `hr_dept_activities_views.xml` uses `target_department_id` and `target_document_item_id` — fields owned by `ticket_system`.

**Fix:** move the `view_department_form_inherit_custom` view (the "Tickets" tab on `hr.department`) into `ticket_system`, where those fields live. Keep only the popup form patch (which only uses `activity_modification` fields) in `employees_department_custom_fields`.

content.text:
Everything looks correct. Here's a summary of the changes made:

**Root cause:** `employees_department_custom_fields` was given `ticket_system` as a dependency (on this branch) to support `target_department_id` / `target_document_item_id` fields in its department activities view. But `ticket_system` depends on `hr_employee_growth` → `employees_department_custom_fields`, creating a cycle that blocked the entire module graph.

**Fix (3 files changed):**

1. **`employees_department_custom_fields/__manifest__.py`** — removed `ticket_system` from `depends`
2. **`employees_department_custom_fields/views/hr_dept_activities_views.xml`** — removed the `view_department_form_inherit_custom` record (the "Tickets" tab), which relied on `ticket_system` fields
3. **`ticket_system/views/hr_department_activities_view.xml`** *(new)* — that view now lives here, where `target_department_id` and `target_document_item_id` are actually defined
4. **`ticket_system/__manifest__.py`** — added the new view file to `data`

The popup form patch (`mail_activity_view_form_popup_inherit_dept`) only uses `activity_modification` fields, so it stays in `employees_department_custom_fields` without issue.

<odoo>
    <record id="view_hr_department_notes_form_inherit" model="ir.ui.view">
        <field name="name">hr.department.form.inherit.notes</field>
        <field name="model">hr.department</field>
        <field name="inherit_id" ref="hr.view_department_form"/>
        <field name="arch" type="xml">
            
            <xpath expr="//page[@name='activities_table_tab']" position="before">
                <page string="Library">
                    <field name="library_item_ids"
                            mode="list,form"
                            nolabel="1">
                        <list>
                        <field name="sequence" widget="handle"/>
                        <field name="name"/>
                        <field name="sharepoint_path"/>
                        <field name="doc_type"/>
                        <field name="tag_ids" widget="many2many_tags"/>
                        <field name="owner_id"/>
                        <field name="status"/>
                        <button name="action_open_sharepoint"
                            type="object"
                            string="Open"
                            icon="fa-external-link"
                            class="btn-link"/>
                        </list>
                    </field>
                </page>

                <page string="Notes" name="department_notes_tab" >
                    <field name="department_notes_ids" context="{'default_department_id': id}">
                        <list editable="bottom">
                            <field name="note_taken_date"/>
                            <field name="note_taken_by" widget="many2one_avatar_user"/>
                            <field name="notes" widget="html" options="{'collaborative': True}"/>
                        </list>
                        
                        <form string="Department Note">
                            <sheet>
                                <group>
                                    <group>
                                        <field name="note_taken_date"/>
                                        <field name="note_taken_by" widget="many2one_avatar_user"/>
                                    </group>
                                </group>
                                <label for="notes" string="Note Content"/>
                                <field name="notes" widget="html" nolabel="1" options="{'style-inline': true}"/>
                            </sheet>
                        </form>
                    </field>
                </page>
            </xpath>

        </field>
    </record>
    <record id="view_department_form_inherit_email" model="ir.ui.view">
        <field name="name">hr.department.form.inherit.email</field>
        <field name="model">hr.department</field>
        <field name="inherit_id" ref="hr.view_department_form"/>
        <field name="arch" type="xml">
            <xpath expr="//field[@name='parent_id']" position="after">
                <field name="dept_email" widget="email"/>
            </xpath>
        </field>
    </record>
</odoo>

message.content:
ticket_system/views/hr_department_activities_view.xml:3
Error while parsing or validating view:

Element '<xpath expr="//page[@name='activities_table_tab']">' cannot be located in parent view but files are updated why is not reflected inside odoo?

content.text:
Odoo stores view definitions in the database — editing the XML files on disk has no effect until the module is upgraded. The database still holds the old view definition.

You need to restart Odoo with the `-u` flag for the affected modules:

```bash
odoo --config=/path/to/odoo.conf -u ticket_system,employees_department_custom_fields
```

Or, if you're running via Docker, something like:

```bash
docker exec <container> odoo -u ticket_system,employees_department_custom_fields
```

Final tab order on `hr.department` form: **Library → Notes → Main Activities → Tickets**

- Library + Notes: created in `employees_department_custom_fields/views/hr_dept_notes_view.xml`
- Main Activities: inserted after Notes in `employees_department_custom_fields/views/hr_dept_main_activities_view.xml`
- Tickets: inserted after Main Activities in `ticket_system/views/hr_department_activities_view.xml`

The branch has commits not on `master` — the cloud is almost certainly running `master` or hasn't pulled the latest. The three new commits (`febd9a1`, `2afdcf7`, `9ea2f86`) only exist on `FEAT-Standarize-Activities`.

You need to do one of these on the cloud server:

**If cloud is on the FEAT branch but just behind:**
```bash
git pull origin FEAT-Standarize-Activities
# then restart Odoo with -u
```

**If cloud is on master (hasn't been merged yet):**
The fix doesn't exist on master yet — you'd need to either merge/cherry-pick or temporarily point the cloud at the FEAT branch.

To check which branch the cloud is actually on, run this on the cloud server:
```bash
git -C /path/to/addons branch --show-current
git -C /path/to/addons log --oneline -3
```

If the latest commit shown isn't `9ea2f86`, the server hasn't received the fix.

Code is there but Odoo's database still has the old view records. The server needs a module upgrade to sync the DB with the new XML.

On the cloud server, restart Odoo with `-u` for the two affected modules:

```bash
odoo -u employees_department_custom_fields,ticket_system -d <your_db_name>
```

Or if running via Docker:
```bash
docker exec <container> odoo -u employees_department_custom_fields,ticket_system -d <your_db_name>
```

Odoo never auto-applies XML changes on restart — it only reads updated files when you explicitly pass `-u`.

- `view`: `hr.department.form.inherit.notes` (id=3814) — still has old xpath `activities_table_tab` in the prod DB
- `xmlid`: `view_department_form_inherit_tickets` — our new view triggered the validation

When `ticket_system` is upgraded, Odoo validates **all** `hr.department` views in the DB. The notes view (not yet updated) still looks for `activities_table_tab`, which doesn't exist at validation time (our new view hasn't been committed yet).

The anchor `//page[@name='main_activities_tab']` is fragile for the same reason — it depends on another module's view state. The fix is to use `//notebook` directly, which is guaranteed to exist once `employees_department_custom_fields` (a dep) is loaded.

content.text:
Now the error is completely clear. The production DB is in a broken intermediate state — `view_department_form_inherit_custom` (which provided `activities_table_tab`) was already removed by a previous failed upgrade, but `view_hr_department_main_activities_form_inherit` (id=4067) still has the old xpath referencing it. Every upgrade attempt hits the same wall.

The only way out of this is a **pre-migration script** that deletes the stale view records before the data files run. Odoo will then recreate them cleanly from the updated XML.
